body:
]> 15/07/2026 7:18 5cm to end the storm and a clear day ahead!.

What should be the last of the storm rolled through last night, and we had another 5cm overnight, bringing the storm total to 33cm. Temps dropped to -3 degrees and the snowguns fired up at around 11pm and are still running this morning to build on the natural snowfall.

The forecast is for a sunny day, with a top of 7 degrees and winds southwesterly 35 to 55 km/h, tending west to southwesterly 20 to 30 km/h in the early afternoon.

We're expecting 21 lifts to spin today, and the wind hold of the last few days should have passed. The groomers will be firm and fast, and with the variable storm temperatures followed by a clear night, I'd expect off-piste to have set up, so recommend waiting for it to soften on the sunny aspects before getting too adventurous.

We have another day of Sydney Interschools on Front Valley and Leichhardt - good luck to everyone competing. Smiggin Holes fireworks are also on tonight at 8pm.

Mountain Ops will be looking to open more lifts and terrain as soon as it's safe to do so. As always, be the first to know by downloading the Perisher App, and keep an eye on the lift status report for real-time updates as lifts may open throughout the day.

]]> 53.4 5 33 21 219 21 *Natural snow depth is supplemented with snowmaking in certain areas. Natural snow depth is measured at roughly 1,830m by Snowy Hydro Limited who have invested in new instrumentation across the Snowy Scheme and can now provide more frequent snow depth readings at a site close to the Spencers Creek snow course. This report reflects conditions at the date and time of this report. Conditions, lifts status and operations subsequent to this report may differ.
